page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, operating at 1.0 thread per core with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 192 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache configuration includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the t2d-standard-48 demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng tests. Single-core performance and PassMark CPU scores are strong, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large block read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. Cryptographic and vector workloads benefit from hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AVX, AES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, image processing, database operations, and Redis deployments, all of which score in the top 25% in benchmarks. It also handles small to medium LLM inference tasks efficiently, though text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10%. Given the dedicated core allocation and balanced resource density, it offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, non-hyperthreaded enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance configured with 48 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7B13 cores and 192 GB of RAM. Operating at one thread per core, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng benchmarks. It features a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, with strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and cryptography. The hardware lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, making it a dedicated compute-and-memory resource. It is highly effective for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, Redis databases, and medium-scale LLM inference, though it exhibits performance trade-offs under large-scale model text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose instance with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 192 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 features 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC platform delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and balanced memory density for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core
192 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10% of benchmarks
Strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and cryptography
Best suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, Redis, and medium LLM inference
